item.title: Version 1.8.8
item.description:
Schedule, now steerable

Week and Month views were stuck showing only the current window — now you can step back and forward through time, and row thumbnails finally show your actual crop.

  • Step through any week or month Week and Month views now have Previous/Next controls plus a Today button, so you're not locked to the current 7 days or calendar month anymore.
  • Thumbnails match your crop Schedule row thumbnails now reflect the framing you set in Edit — cropped and padded posts finally look the way they'll publish, matching the Library card.

item.title: Version 1.8.7
item.description:
iPhone's Edit sheet, tuned for touch

Every tool in the iPhone Edit sheet now works the way a Lightroom-mobile editor should — your photo pinned in place at the top, the tools scrolling underneath.

  • Your photo stays put Crop, Matte, Panorama, and Text post all now pin the photo at the top while their tools scroll beneath it — no more losing your place.
  • Built to match the Mac Same tools, same controls, same result — just laid out the way a phone actually wants to be held.

Every brand, a face

Brands can now carry a real photo and a color — set them once in Settings, see them everywhere a brand shows up.

  • Upload a photo, frame it yourself Add a profile picture to any brand, then drag to move and slide to zoom inside the frame — exactly what you see is what gets saved.
  • A color for every brand Pick each brand's color right alongside its photo in Settings → Brands — it's what shows when there's no picture yet.
  • It shows up everywhere The brand switcher now shows the photo instead of a plain color dot, wherever there's room to show it.
  • Edit it from your phone too iPhone can now set a brand's photo and color directly — its own crop-and-zoom sheet, matching the Mac exactly.

Text posts, one Edit away

Text posts now stylize the same way everything else does — open Edit, shape the card, and export once. No more separate export button to remember.

  • Stylize, then export — once Open Edit on a text post and shape it in the new Text post tab — background, size, tone. One Export button bakes the branded image automatically, whenever a photo-first platform is riding along.
  • A card that fits its words The caption now shrinks to fit the card instead of clipping, with a cleaner icon mark in the corner and sizing tuned for how Threads actually crops it.
  • More room to work The Mac Edit sheet now puts your canvas on the left and every tool on the right, on every tab.

iPhone gets its own welcome

First time opening Persona on iPhone? You'll get a quick tour of what's here. After that, every update brings its own What's New — the same release notes you'd see on Mac, now on your phone too.

  • A proper hello, the first time Open Persona on iPhone for the first time and get a quick walk-through — your brands, your schedule, each brand's voice, and how to capture a post on the go.
  • What's new, right on your phone After an update, see what changed without leaving iPhone — the same highlights the Mac shows, in the same voice.
  • The whole history, whenever you want it Settings now holds every past update, plus a way to replay the welcome tour any time you want a refresher.

Voice — your brand's bible

The Creative Profile page is now Voice: a brand's bible — the one page an AI reads before it writes in its voice. Capture how a brand sounds, what it stands for, and what it never does, with a living index that shows how complete each chapter is.

  • One page that defines your voice The Creative Profile is now Voice — a brand bible organized into chapters: Identity, Voice, Substance, Guardrails, Channels, and Examples. A living index down the left tracks how much of each you've filled in, so you can see the whole picture and what's still missing.
  • Say how it sounds — and what it never does Describe the voice in plain words, place it on a tone scale from Formal to Playful, and spell out the words you use, the ones you never do, your do's and don'ts, and the topics to steer clear of. The AI writes inside those lines.
  • See it the way the AI does A live preview at the bottom shows the exact page an AI reads before it writes for you — so what you put in is precisely what it works from.
  • A calm read on your phone On iPhone and iPad your bible is a clean, read-only read — authoring stays on the Mac, where there's room to think.

item.title: Version 1.8.2
item.description:
A smoother connection to Claude

Updating Persona's Claude extension now confirms itself on its own — and the app goes by Persona everywhere it shows up.

  • The Claude extension update just works When you update Persona's Claude extension, the panel now flips to “Installed” on its own the moment it's done — no more backing out of Settings and reopening it to see that it went through.
  • Now just Persona The app now shows up as “Persona” in Spotlight, the Dock, and search — matching the name it already used inside. Fresh installs pick this up automatically.

item.title: Version 1.8.1
item.description:
A brief with room to breathe

The brief now grows as you type and scrolls when it fills up — the same roomy, caption-style box — so you can capture the whole story behind a post.

  • Room to write your brief The brief field now grows as you type and starts scrolling once it fills, just like your caption box. Spell out the full intent behind a post — what it's really about, who it's for, the angle you're going for — instead of squeezing it onto a line or two.
  • More of it in quick capture When you jot down a quick brief, more of what you wrote now stays visible before it scrolls — so a longer note is readable at a glance without opening the whole post.

item.title: Version 1.8.0
item.description:
The Edit page — crop, matte, and split panoramas

Crop, Matte, and Panorama now live together in one Edit sheet — reframe a photo, drop it on a colored background, or split a wide shot into a carousel, all from Mac or iPhone.

  • One Edit page for everything Crop, Matte, and Panorama are now tabs in a single Edit sheet instead of separate tools — open it once and switch between them without losing your place. Works the same way on Mac and iPhone.
  • Matte any photo or video Fit a photo or video onto a colored background instead of cropping it away — pick your color, dial in the border, and it composes cleanly with a crop underneath. Works for video too, not just photos.
  • Split a wide photo into a carousel Turn a panorama into 2 to 6 seamless carousel slides with one tap. Not happy with the split? Revert it losslessly and try again — your original photo is always right there.
  • More reliable when editing across devices Edits made on your iPhone now merge correctly with a Mac library that's open at the same time, so a split or a crop never gets silently reverted.
  • Various bug fixes Plus fixes for carousel swiping and how panoramas show up in your Library.

item.title: Version 1.7.0
item.description:
Meet Persona

Content Strategy is now Persona — the same app and the same library you already have, with a fresh name and a new face for the work you put forward.

  • A new name, a new face Content Strategy is now Persona. It's the same app you've been using — every brand, post, and plan is right where you left it — with a new name, a new icon, and a refined identity throughout.
  • Everything is right where you left it Nothing moved and nothing changed but the name and the look. All of your brands, posts, media, and schedules are exactly as they were — Persona reads your library the same way it always has.

item.title: Version 1.6.0
item.description:
Crop, reframe, and cleaner carousels

A brand-new Crop tool lets you frame every photo exactly how you want on Mac and iPhone, while carousels finally swipe and preview true to shape.

  • Crop and reframe your photos A new Crop tool lets you pick a shape and reposition each photo inside it — the full image stays in view while you move and resize the frame right on top of it. Drag to move, use the zoom dial or pinch to tighten, and reopen a crop anytime to pick up right where you left off. Your original files are never changed.
  • Frame each carousel photo, on any device Step through a carousel and reframe every photo on its own so the whole set lines up beautifully — on your Mac or right from your iPhone. Choose a standard shape or go Custom to pull the corners into anything you like, and the whole carousel snaps to that same shape to stay uniform.
  • Cleaner carousel swiping Swiping through a multi-photo post now snaps neatly from slide to slide with no half-image seams, opens squarely on the first image instead of stalling between two, and the iPhone swipe that used to freeze is smooth again.
  • True-to-shape previews everywhere Every carousel slide now shows the exact crop it'll publish with — matching across your Library cards, the post view, and your phone. Tall vertical photos finally show full-height, and posts set to Auto frame the same way on every screen.
  • Various bug fixes Plus a handful of smaller fixes and refinements throughout the app.

item.title: Version 1.5.1
item.description:
A quiet fix

The Recently Deleted indicator could get stuck on even after there was nothing left to recover. It clears properly now.

item.title: Version 1.5.0
item.description:
Recently Deleted, right where you need it

Recovering a deleted post is easier to find, and your phone and iPad just caught up to your Mac's Library.

  • Recently Deleted moved into the Library toolbar — instead of a sidebar row, look for the clock icon next to Library's other tools. It lights up the moment something's waiting to be recovered, and now respects whichever brand you're filtering to.
  • Your phone and iPad caught up — the Library toolbar (filter by status or date, sort newest or oldest, select multiple posts to delete or change status at once) is now on iPhone and iPad too, along with Recently Deleted for recovering anything you remove.

item.title: Version 1.4.1
item.description:
Cleaner syncing between devices

Edits made on different devices at the same time now merge more precisely, and the Claude integration only asks for an update when it actually needs one.

item.title: Version 1.4.0
item.description:
A faster way to work your Library

Find and tidy your posts in seconds, and lock in timing right as you compose.

  • Filter your Library Narrow your Library to just drafts, scheduled, or published posts, or to a specific date range — and it remembers your choice next time you open up. Everything you've ever published is easy to find again, too.
  • Handle posts in batches Select several posts at once to delete them together or set their status in one move, so tidying up takes seconds instead of one post at a time.
  • Schedule while you compose Set a post's status right from the clean compose screen — pick Scheduled and a date picker slides in so you can lock the timing up front, no need to open the full editor first.

item.title: Version 1.3.6
item.description:
Claude knows your library better

A behind-the-scenes tune-up so Claude understands how your content is organized the moment it connects. Nothing to set up — it just works a little smarter.

  • Smarter out of the box When you connect your library to Claude, it now arrives already understanding how things fit together — brands, posts, scheduling, and your creative profile — so it stays on-brand and asks before deleting anything.

item.title: Version 1.3.5
item.description:
One-click Claude, everywhere

Connecting your library to Claude is now a single click — and it shows up in both Cowork and Claude Desktop chats.

  • Connect to Claude in one click Add your library to Claude in a single click, and it now shows up in both Cowork and regular Claude Desktop chats. From there Claude can work with your content directly — list brands, schedule posts, attach media, and more.
  • Stays in sync as you update When a new version moves ahead of your Claude connection, a small marker appears in Settings so you can refresh it in one tap and keep everything talking.

item.title: Version 1.3.4
item.description:
A rare crash, quietly closed

A stability fix for a startup hiccup that could affect libraries synced across multiple Macs. If you never hit it, you won't notice a thing — which is exactly the point.

  • Steadier on launch Closed a rare case where a duplicate brand record left behind by iCloud sync could stop the app from opening. It now tidies the duplicate on the way up and starts clean.

item.title: Version 1.3.3
item.description:
A cleaner, calmer Library

A visual tune-up across the Library and sidebar — tighter, lighter, and more like a proper workstation, so your content does the talking.

  • A tighter, lighter sidebar The navigation rail slimmed down — smaller type, less padding, and a quieter “Recently Deleted” tucked at the bottom — so it reads like a focused workspace instead of a heavy menu bar.
  • Cards that show more at a glance Library cards are a touch smaller with a cleaner title and quieter status pills, so more of your work fits on screen without the chrome getting in the way.
  • Landscape and text posts fit right Wide 16:9 and YouTube-shaped posts now stretch across two columns so they carry real weight, and text-only posts render as wide, tidy cards that read like a social post.

item.title: Version 1.3.2
item.description:
A lighter footprint

Another one for the "you'll never notice, and that's the point" pile. We trimmed how much the app writes to disk and how loudly it talks to itself behind the scenes — so it stays lean and leaves your iCloud alone unless something actually changed.

  • Writes less, syncs cleaner The app now skips re-saving anything that hasn't truly changed, so it stops giving iCloud reasons to spawn duplicate copies of your posts. Quieter syncing, fewer stray files.
  • Tidier under the hood We put a lid on the app's internal diagnostic log and calmed a chatty background process, so things stay light even when iCloud is having a slow day.

item.title: Version 1.3.1
item.description:
A quiet tune-up

No new buttons in this one — just the satisfying kind of update. We made things faster and tougher under the hood so the app stays out of your way and you can get back to creating. You don't have to read this one… but we're glad you did.

  • Bye-bye, beach balls Photos and thumbnails now load quietly in the background instead of freezing things up while iCloud catches up. Scroll your Library, flip through a carousel — it just keeps pace.
  • Quietly bulletproofed We tightened how the app and your AI assistant share your library, so two edits landing at the same moment can never step on each other. You'll never see it work — which is exactly the point.

item.title: Version 1.3.0
item.description:
The composer, reimagined

A redesigned composer, three new platforms, text-only posts, and a tap-to-copy caption handoff make this the biggest content release yet.

  • A roomier place to write Your caption now sits in a wide column right under your photos and grows as you type, so long captions never feel cramped. The whole composer is cleaner — your media is centered, your title and publishing controls stay tidy on the right, and platform previews stretch across the full width below.
  • Plan for YouTube, Threads & X Three new platforms join the lineup — YouTube long-form, Threads, and X — each with its own live preview so you can see exactly how a post will look. Flip on X Premium for any brand that pays for it and your caption counter follows along, allowing up to 25,000 characters. Each brand keeps its own setting.
  • Post text on its own A caption is now a complete post all by itself — no photo or video required. Write the words you want to share and schedule or export them like any other post, and they show up in your Library as a clean, phone-style text card instead of a blank placeholder.
  • Tailor platforms per brand Each brand can now turn platforms on or off in its Creative Profile, so the composer only ever offers the channels that brand actually posts to. New posts no longer pre-pick a platform either — you decide where each one goes, and disabled buttons tell you exactly what's still needed before you can schedule.
  • Tap to copy your captions Exporting a post now gives you one clean caption page you can open right on your phone and tap to copy each platform's caption, hashtags, and first comment — plus a PDF and per-platform text files. A new Copy button in the app grabs any version in a single click, and what you copy is exactly what shows up in your export.
  • Smarter caption limits Each platform now counts the caption it will actually post and warns you when it runs too long, even on platforms you never customized. TikTok's limit is corrected too, so you'll know before a video caption runs over.
  • Move through the app faster Press Command-N from any screen to jump straight into a fresh post — and if you start something new while a post is still in progress, the app offers to save it as a draft first so your work is never lost. A two-finger swipe right on your trackpad now takes you back out of a post, just like on iPhone and iPad.
  • Smoother and more polished Your Library now glides gracefully as cards rearrange when you open or close the sidebar, photos stay neatly in place above the caption, and the occasional spinning beach ball is gone — thumbnails load faster so scrolling and typing stay snappy throughout.

item.title: Version 1.2.0
item.description:
What's New, in the app

See exactly what changed in every release — and never get stuck on an old version again.

  • A What's New window. Browse every release in one place: pick a version on the left, scroll through all the changes on the right. Open it any time from the app menu or Settings.
  • Updates that just happen. New versions now download quietly in the background. When one's ready, the sidebar shows it with the version number — click to relaunch and you're current.
  • Always up to date. The app checks for updates far more often now, including when you come back to it — so you're never quietly running an old build.

item.title: Version 1.1.3
item.description:
v1.1.3 — Smarter captions & a cleaner title bar

What changed

  • Caption length cautions. The caption counter flags when you're over a platform's limit — Instagram 2,200, TikTok 4,000, YouTube Shorts 5,000 — and names exactly which platform. Stories have no caption limit, and nothing is ever blocked; you can always keep typing.
  • A real caption editor. The caption now scrolls and takes line breaks — press Return for a new line, and scroll the caption itself when your pointer is over it.
  • Cleaner title bar. Scrolling content fades neatly behind the title bar instead of colliding with the brand switcher and page actions.
  • Plus: hover feedback on Settings & Feedback buttons, the + button opens a new post from any page, and a quieter folder sync.

item.title: Version 1.1.2
item.description:
v1.1.2 — A cleaner window & quicker edits

What changed

  • Fixed, non-floating sidebar. The left rail is now a flush panel instead of a floating glass overlay — cleaner and more like a native Mac app.
  • Comfortable title bar. The window controls sit with proper breathing room and are borderless, not boxed into a glass button.
  • Post actions on the page. Back, Export, and Schedule Post now live at the top of the post itself instead of overlapping the menu bar.
  • Delete one carousel slide. Right-click any asset in a carousel filmstrip → Remove from Post.
  • Click anywhere to stop editing. While editing a post, click any empty space to dismiss the text cursor.
  • Tighter, better-aligned page spacing throughout.

item.title: Version 1.1.1
item.description:
v1.1.1 — Carousel ratios + a redesigned Library

What changed

  • Carousel ratios. Lock a multi-photo post to a platform-standard shape (4:5 · 1:1 · 1.91:1 · 9:16) so what you plan in the app matches what actually publishes. The picker only offers the ratios valid for the platforms you're targeting, and the choice frames the card, the post preview, and every slide consistently.
  • Redesigned Library. A flowing, Pinterest-style gallery — more posts at a glance, consistent sizing (two posts of the same shape are always the same size), and it reflows live as you resize the window or open the sidebar.
  • Polish. Smoother selection animations, a snappier cover-art frame picker, clearer Schedule day dividers, and a copy confirmation in Feedback.

item.title: Version 1.1.0
item.description:
v1.1.0 — Local-first folder library + redesigned compose

Your library is now a real folder you can see in Finder / iCloud Drive — each brand is a folder, each post a folder with a readable post.json plus its media. Your content lives as files you own, not inside an opaque sync database.

What changed

  • Local-first folder library. The folder is canonical; the internal database is now just a disposable index rebuilt from the folder.
  • Cross-Mac sync rides iCloud Drive (the reliable layer) instead of the CloudKit metadata database that was wedging with the persistent "service unavailable" error.
  • Redesigned new-post compose — clean slate, your last brand pre-selected, media attaches in place, ⌘↩ to Save and ⇧⌘↩ to Save & New.
  • Extensive data-safety hardening from dogfood: provenance-flagged deletes, debounced autosave, conflict-copy cleanup, and live cross-Mac delete reconciliation.

First install of v1.1.0 migrates your existing library into the new folder format automatically; your original data is left untouched as a rollback safety net.

item.title: Version 1.0.15
item.description:
v1.0.15 — Sidebar fix + iCloud reconnect helper

Fixes

  • Sidebar items render correctly. v1.0.14 inadvertently pushed the sidebar's Library / Schedule / Creative Profile / Settings items out of the visible viewport. Fixed.
  • "Reconnect iCloud" recovery affordance. When CloudKit setup fails persistently (the "service unavailable" error that's been hitting Tiera's Mac and any post-Reset Mac), the empty-state card now surfaces a one-click Reconnect iCloud deep-link to System Settings → Apple ID → iCloud, where the user-side fix lives (toggle Content Strategy off and on under Apps Using iCloud, then quit and reopen). Same affordance also surfaces in Settings → iCloud Sync whenever sync is in Error or Sync paused state. The card's body copy walks through the recovery sequence.

About v1.0.13 + v1.0.14

Both prior releases shipped with regressions (window stretch + misleading Welcome card on v1.0.13; sidebar items pushed offscreen on v1.0.14). They've been removed from the Sparkle appcast — Sparkle no longer advertises them. Existing v1.0.13 / v1.0.14 installs stay where they are; this v1.0.15 supersedes both via the normal Sparkle update path.

item.title: Version 1.0.12
item.description:
v1.0.12 — Update banner moved to the sidebar + honest sync state

Update affordance

  • "Update available" pill moved from the top of the detail column to the bottom of the sidebar, in rust. Click it to install + relaunch. Same trigger logic; just lives in a single, predictable spot regardless of which view is showing.

iCloud Sync (Settings → iCloud Sync)

  • Honest "Sync paused" state. Earlier builds could show a green "Synced" pill even when CloudKit had failed to bootstrap on this Mac (no successful sync had ever happened). v1.0.12 surfaces an amber Sync paused pill in that case with an actionable sub-message — no more silent lying.
  • New "Reset local cache" button. Destructive action with a confirmation dialog. Quits the app and wipes the local SwiftData store on next launch, forcing a fresh CloudKit re-bootstrap. Use it if iCloud sync looks stuck on this Mac. Local-only changes that haven't synced will be lost.
  • Bug reports submitted via Settings → Send feedback now include bootstrapErrorCount and lastBootstrapError in the diagnostic snapshot, so we can finally tell from a debug report whether a Mac actually completed CloudKit bootstrap or quietly stalled.

Under the hood

  • MCP server (Settings → Connect to Claude) now reports its version dynamically from the bundle instead of a hardcoded literal.

item.title: Version 1.0.11
item.description:
v1.0.11 — Library card + video player polish, autocorrect

Library

  • Solo wide cards capped at 720pt and aspect-clamped 4:5–16:9 (no more 16:9 sprawl)
  • Carousel posts let you trackpad-swipe through assets right in the Library card with page-indicator dots
  • Cover art (when set) wins over first-asset for the card thumbnail
  • Thumbnail resolution bumped 512px → 1024px for retina sharpness; existing thumbnails regenerate once on next launch

Post detail

  • Hero block is a paging swipe between assets (Instagram-style); page dots at the bottom
  • Tapping a filmstrip tile navigates the pager (no longer mutates the post's asset order)
  • Drag-reorder shows an insert-gap bar between tiles to telegraph where the dragged item will land
  • Inline video player: auto-mute + auto-play + seamless loop, IG-style mute toggle bottom-right (always visible), hover reveals play/pause + scrubber + duration

Captions / text input

  • New .prose / .identifier / .token role system applied across every text field
  • Captions, briefs, voice description, post titles, feedback bodies → spellcheck/autocorrect ON
  • Hashtags, mentions, brand handles, channel handles → autocorrect OFF

Platform previews

  • Caption text now white + drop-shadowed (was invisible against bright photos in light mode)
  • Play glyph centered in the device frame
  • Image fill simplified to match Library card pattern

item.title: Version 1.0.10
item.description:
v1.0.10 — actual Sparkle sandbox fix (both halves this time)

  • The complete Sparkle sandbox fix. Sparkle 2.x sandboxed install requires TWO XPC mechanisms in tandem — bundled XPC services (Installer.xpc + Downloader.xpc) AND mach-named XPC services registered by the Autoupdate helper. v1.0.5 had partial #2 only. v1.0.8/v1.0.9 had #1 only. v1.0.10 has both.
  • Verified by reading Sparkle source: SPUInstallerDriver.m:210, SUInstallerLauncher.m:286, AppInstaller.m:120, Autoupdate/SPUMessageTypes.m:16-18.
  • Empirical verification deferred. v1.0.9 → v1.0.10 will NOT auto-update (v1.0.9 has the same bug). Manual DMG install one last time; the next ship's auto-update from a v1.0.10 install is the actual empirical proof.
  • Everything from v1.0.9 carries forward (one-click Claude integration, sync polish, container ID drop).

item.title: Version 1.0.9
item.description:
v1.0.9 — One-click Claude integration + sync polish

  • One-click Claude integration setup. Settings → Connect to Claude now has a single "Connect Content Strategy to Claude" button. Click it once; the app writes the MCP entry into Claude Desktop's config for you — no JSON, no copy-paste. Disconnect button removes the entry the same way. Manual snippet flow stays behind a "Show advanced" disclosure for non-Claude MCP clients.
  • Opt-in by design. The app NEVER writes Claude's config without an explicit Settings-panel click. Every write backs up the existing config to a .bak-cs-{timestamp} file first.
  • No more misleading "Error" at startup. The Settings → iCloud Sync section used to show a red "Error" pill for the first 10–20s of every launch (transient CloudKit bootstrap errors). v1.0.9 shows "Connecting" until the first successful event, then transitions normally.
  • Removed the iCloud.com.kamstudios.ContentStrategy container ID line from the iCloud Sync section. Debug noise — container ID stays in bug reports for triage.

item.title: Version 1.0.8
item.description:
v1.0.8 — actual Sparkle sandbox fix

  • In-app auto-update now actually works for sandboxed installs. v1.0.5 / 1.0.6 / 1.0.7 all shipped with the wrong fix (a mach-lookup entitlement that did nothing for bundled XPC services). The real fix: two Info.plist keys (SUEnableInstallerLauncherService + SUEnableDownloaderService) that flip Sparkle's runtime checks to use its bundled XPC services via NSXPCConnection initWithServiceName: instead of the in-process installer that hits Authorization Services (sandbox-blocked, errAuthorizationDenied -60005).
  • Confirmed by reading Sparkle's source directly. v1.0.8 verified working before publish.
  • v1.0.7 and earlier sandboxed installs cannot auto-update past this gap. Manual install via the DMG one last time; v1.0.8 onward auto-updates cleanly.
  • Everything from v1.0.7 carries forward (Settings → Connect to Claude + Try-asking-Claude primer + Feedback routing visibility).

item.title: Version 1.0.7
item.description:
v1.0.7 — Connect to Claude + Feedback routing visibility

  • One-stop Claude integration setup. Settings → Connect to Claude hands you the MCP snippet for claude_desktop_config.json, with copy / reveal-config / verify-registration actions. Plus a Try asking Claude primer with five example prompts so you don't need docs to start using it.
  • Feedback routing is now honest about what happened. Reports that fall through to local fallback (because the iCloud share isn't on this Mac) now show in amber instead of pretending they synced. Settings → Feedback adds an Open iCloud Folder button + conditional "Show local fallback (N files)" diagnostic.
  • FeedbackService logs the attempted iCloud path + sharedParentExists on every submission for future debugging.

item.title: Version 1.0.6
item.description:
v1.0.6 — orphan-migration crash fix

  • Fixes a SwiftData crash on launch in v1.0.5. The session-30 orphan-CreativeProfile migration raced with CloudKit's eventual-consistency relationship sync — when CloudKit was mid-pull, profiles briefly appeared as brand == nil, the migration tried to attach them, and SwiftData asserted because the profile was already CloudKit-bound. The migration was a one-shot for v1.0.x → v1.0.3; anyone with real orphans was already migrated by their v1.0.3 install. Removed in v1.0.6.
  • Carries forward from v1.0.4 + v1.0.5: Library-as-MCP-server (7 tools) and Sparkle sandbox auto-update fix.

item.title: Version 1.0.5
item.description:
v1.0.5 — Sparkle sandbox auth fix + MCP server

  • Auto-update from inside the app now works. v1.0.4 shipped without the com.apple.security.temporary-exception.mach-lookup.global-name entitlements Sparkle 2.x needs in sandboxed apps. v1.0.4 (and earlier sandboxed installs) can detect this update but can't install it from inside the app — manual install required one last time via the GitHub Releases page.
  • Library-as-MCP-server from v1.0.4 carries forward. Connect Claude Code via {"mcpServers":{"content-strategy":{"command":"/Applications/ContentStrategy.app/Contents/Resources/ContentStrategyMCP.app/Contents/MacOS/ContentStrategyMCP"}}} in your ~/.claude/settings.local.json.

item.title: Version 1.0.4
item.description:
v1.0.4 — Library-as-MCP-server

  • Library-as-MCP-server. Connect Claude Code (or Claude Desktop) directly to your Content Strategy library. Seven tools — list_brands, list_posts, read_creative_profile, create_post, attach_media, update_post, delete_post — let Claude read and write your content via stdio.
  • Setup: after install, add an MCP server entry pointing at /Applications/ContentStrategy.app/Contents/Resources/ContentStrategyMCP.app/Contents/MacOS/ContentStrategyMCP in your Claude Code config, then reload. The seven tools appear as MCP tools.
  • Writes from Claude appear in the running app within ~30 seconds via CloudKit sync. Both processes share the same iCloud + CloudKit private database as the app.

item.title: Version 1.0.3
item.description:
v1.0.3 — Cross-Mac sync + per-brand Creative Profile + editable handles

  • Cross-Mac sync. Posts, Brands, Assets, and Creative Profile metadata now sync across your Macs through CloudKit's private database. Media files keep syncing through iCloud Drive (already shipped). Fresh installs see a "Syncing your content from iCloud…" card while the data lands.
  • Per-brand Creative Profile. Each brand now has its own voice, tone, audience, channel handles, and guardrails. Switch brands from the title-bar picker while on the Creative Profile page. Existing Creative Profiles auto-attach to your earliest-created brand on first launch.
  • Editable brand handles. Settings → Brands → three-dot menu → "Edit handle" lets you change the @handle on any brand inline.
  • Welcome onboarding. Fresh installs see a "Let's set up your first brand" card instead of an empty Library.

item.title: Version 1.0.2
item.description:
v1.0.2 — Sparkle SUFeedURL fix + shared feedback folder

  • Sparkle auto-update now actually works. v1.0 and v1.0.1 shipped without SUFeedURL in their Info.plist — auto-update was silently broken. Fixed in v1.0.2 by switching to a real custom Info.plist.
  • Feedback files land in the shared iCloud folder. Reports go to Kennedy Family Docs/Claude/Content Strategy Feedback/ — both Kam and Tiera see each other's reports.

v1.0/v1.0.1 installs need to manually download v1.0.2 one more time (their broken Sparkle can't fetch this appcast). After v1.0.2, every future update auto-delivers.

item.title: Version 1.0.1
item.description:
v1.0.1 — first-launch + auto-update banner

  • Fixes the + button being disabled on first install (no brands → no entry point). You can now click + on a brandless install; the inline brand picker prompts you to create your first brand.
  • Adds an inline auto-update banner so future updates surface as "Install Now / Later" inside the app instead of Sparkle's default sheet.

item.title: Version 1.0
item.description:
v1.0 — first alpha

First internal alpha drop. Use Settings → Send feedback to report issues; reports save into your iCloud Content Strategy folder.
